citations: [Extracellular matrix stiffness regulates colorectal cancer progression via HSF4.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39881364/)

evidence.claim:

evidence.source_title: Extracellular matrix stiffness regulates colorectal cancer progression via HSF4.
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 39881364. 2025/01/30 00:00. BACKGROUND: Colorectal cancer (CRC) has high incidence and mortality rates, with 
severe prognoses during invasion and metastasis stages. Despite advancements in 
diagnostic and therapeutic technologies, the impact of the tumour 
microenvironment, particularly extracellular matrix (ECM) stiffness, on CRC 
progression and metastasis is not fully understood.
METHODS: This study included 107 CRC patients. Tumour stiffness was assessed 
using magnetic resonance
evidence.search_query: NRC regulations medical physicist presence HDR brachytherapy treatment delivery requirements
